autocomplete.title: Basic usage
autocomplete.description: The following example illustrates use of a m3e-autocomplete in conjunction with the m3e-form-field. See Form Field for more information.
autocomplete.code:
<m3e-form-field>
  <label slot="label" for="fruit">Choose your favorite fruit</label>
  <input id="fruit">
</m3e-form-field>
<m3e-autocomplete for="fruit">
  <m3e-option>Apples</m3e-option>
  <m3e-option>Oranges</m3e-option>
  <m3e-option>Bananas</m3e-option>
  <m3e-option>Grapes</m3e-option>
</m3e-autocomplete>
autocomplete.brevitySource:
<m3e-form-field>
  <label slot="label" for="fruit">Choose your favorite fruit</label>
  <input id="fruit" />
</m3e-form-field>
<m3e-autocomplete for="fruit">
  <m3e-option>Apples</m3e-option>
  <m3e-option>Oranges</m3e-option>
  <m3e-option>Bananas</m3e-option>
  <m3e-option>Grapes</m3e-option>
</m3e-autocomplete>

  <m3e-option>Bananas</m3e-option>

autocomplete.title: No data
autocomplete.description: Use the no-data-label attribute to control the text announced and displayed when no options match. The no-data slot can be used to provide custom visual content for the empty state. Alternatively, use the hide-no-data attribute to suppress the empty-state UI entirely.
autocomplete.code:
<m3e-form-field>
  <label slot="label" for="fruit6">Choose your favorite fruit</label>
  <input id="fruit6" value="Pear">
</m3e-form-field>
<m3e-autocomplete for="fruit6" no-data-label="No data">
  <m3e-option>Apples</m3e-option>
  <m3e-option>Oranges</m3e-option>
  <m3e-option>Bananas</m3e-option>
  <m3e-option>Grapes</m3e-option>

autocomplete.title: Initial load
autocomplete.description: The following example illustrates lazy loading initial data when the input is focused. In addition, a m3e-loading-indicator is presented in the loading slot. See Loading Indicator for more information.
autocomplete.code:
<m3e-form-field>
  <label slot="label" for="state">State</label>
  <input id="state">
</m3e-form-field>
<m3e-autocomplete class="lazy" for="state">
  <m3e-loading-indicator slot="loading"></m3e-loading-indicator>
</m3e-autocomplete>

autocomplete.title: Search as you type
autocomplete.description: The next example performs a search only after the user pauses typing. The loading indicator is not shown while the user is typing and appears only when an actual search request is made. If the user begins typing while a search is in progress, the loading indicator remains visible, the in-flight request is canceled, and a new search is started after debouncing. This example sets filter="none" to disable built-in filtering.
autocomplete.code:
<m3e-form-field>
  <label slot="label" for="state2">State</label>
  <input id="state2">
</m3e-form-field>
<m3e-autocomplete class="search" for="state2" filter="none">
  <m3e-loading-indicator slot="loading"></m3e-loading-indicator>
</m3e-autocomplete>
autocomplete.brevitySource:
<m3e-form-field>
  <label slot="label" for="state">State</label>
  <input id="state" />
</m3e-form-field>
<m3e-autocomplete for="state" filter="none" class="search">
</m3e-autocomplete>

bottom-sheet.title: Basic usage
bottom-sheet.description: Use the m3e-bottom-sheet to present content anchored to the bottom of the screen. Bottom sheets are closed by default. You can open a sheet using a m3e-bottom-sheet-trigger nested inside any clickable element. You can also open a sheet declaratively with the open attribute or programmatically with the show method. To close a sheet, use a m3e-bottom-sheet-action inside a clickable element, remove the open attribute, or call the hide method programmatically.
bottom-sheet.code: Open bottom sheet Google Keep Add to a note Google Docs Embed in a document
bottom-sheet.brevitySource: Open bottom sheet Google Keep Add to a note Google Docs Embed in a document

bottom-sheet.title: Variants
bottom-sheet.description: Bottom sheets are standard by default. Add the modal attribute to m3e-bottom-sheet to create a modal bottom sheet.
bottom-sheet.code: Open modal bottom sheet Google Keep Add to a note Google Docs Embed in a document
bottom-sheet.brevitySource:
<!-- Trigger omitted for brevity -->
<m3e-bottom-sheet id="bottomSheet" modal>
    <!-- Content omitted for brevity -->
</m3e-bottom-sheet>

bottom-sheet.title: Drag handles
bottom-sheet.description: Add the handle attribute to m3e-bottom-sheet to display the drag handle and use the handle-label attribute to provide an accessible label (defaults to "Drag handle").

bottom-sheet.brevitySource:
<!-- Trigger omitted for brevity -->

    <!-- Content omitted for brevity -->
</m3e-bottom-sheet>

bottom-sheet.title: Detents
bottom-sheet.description: Detents are declared using the detents attribute on m3e-bottom-sheet. Each detent represents a target height the sheet can snap to.
bottom-sheet.code: Open draggable modal bottom sheet with detents Google Keep Add to a note Google Docs Embed in a document
bottom-sheet.brevitySource:
<!-- Trigger omitted for brevity -->
<m3e-bottom-sheet id="bottomSheet" modal handle detents="fit half full">
    <!-- Content omitted for brevity -->
</m3e-bottom-sheet>

bottom-sheet.title: Initial height
bottom-sheet.description: You can also set the initial detent on a m3e-bottom-sheet-trigger using its detent attribute, allowing each trigger to open the sheet at a different height.

    <!-- Content omitted for brevity -->
</m3e-bottom-sheet>

bottom-sheet.title: Collapsed height
bottom-sheet.description: When the sheet is collapsed, the content area is always marked inert. This applies whenever the sheet's rendered height falls at or below the combined height of the header and the configured peek height. In this state, only the header is interactive; the content cannot receive focus, pointer events, or screen-reader navigation. This prevents users from interacting with partially hidden elements and ensures the collapsed state behaves as a non-interactive preview. The content becomes interactive again only after the sheet expands above the collapsed height.
bottom-sheet.code: Open draggable modal bottom sheet with detents at collapsed with custom peek Google Keep Add to a note Google Docs Embed in a document
bottom-sheet.brevitySource:
<!-- Trigger omitted for brevity -->
<m3e-bottom-sheet 
  id="bottomSheet"
  modal 
  handle 
  detents="collapsed fit half full"
  style="--m3e-bottom-sheet-peek-height: 36px">
  <!-- Content omitted for brevity -->
</m3e-bottom-sheet>

bottom-sheet.title: Hideability
bottom-sheet.description: A bottom sheet is hideable when it can be dismissed by dragging downward past its lowest detent. This allows the sheet to behave like a modal dialog or a temporary surface that can be fully removed from view. You enable this behavior by adding the hideable attribute to m3e-bottom-sheet.

bottom-sheet.brevitySource:

<m3e-bottom-sheet id="bottomSheet" modal handle hideable>
    <!-- Content omitted for brevity -->
</m3e-bottom-sheet>

bottom-sheet.title: Headers
bottom-sheet.description: If the handle is not enabled, the entire header region—including any slotted header content—remains draggable. This ensures the sheet always preserves a reliable drag surface regardless of whether a handle is present. If dragging is undesired, place header content in the default slot and omit the handle attribute.

breadcrumb.title: Anatomy
breadcrumb.description: To create a breadcrumb trail, place your items inside an m3e-breadcrumb element. Each m3e-breadcrumb-item represents one level in the navigation hierarchy, starting with the broadest level and ending with the most specific. The final item is treated as the current level automatically, while the earlier items describe the path that leads to it.

breadcrumb.title: Links
breadcrumb.description: Breadcrumb items can also behave like links by specifying a URL using the href attribute. You can also specify target, and rel similar to native anchor tags. Links are not supported for the last item in a breadcrumb because the current level is not interactive.
breadcrumb.code:
<m3e-breadcrumb>
  <m3e-breadcrumb-item href="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web" target="_blank">Web</m3e-breadcrumb-item>
  <!-- Additional items omitted for brevity -->
</m3e-breadcrumb>

breadcrumb.title: Icons
breadcrumb.description: When using m3e-icon, the icon in the current breadcrumb item will automatically be filled.
breadcrumb.brevitySource:
<m3e-breadcrumb>
  <m3e-breadcrumb-item item-label="Dashboard">
    <m3e-icon name="dashboard"></m3e-icon>
  </m3e-breadcrumb-item>
  <m3e-breadcrumb-item>
    <m3e-icon slot="icon" name="analytics"></m3e-icon>Reports
  </m3e-breadcrumb-item>
  <m3e-breadcrumb-item>
    <m3e-icon slot="icon" name="calendar_month"></m3e-icon>Annual
  </m3e-breadcrumb-item>
</m3e-breadcrumb>

breadcrumb.title: Custom separators
breadcrumb.description: Use the separator slot to provide a custom visual separator. The slotted element is cloned into each item's shadow root, replacing the default separator.
breadcrumb.brevitySource: / Dashboard

breadcrumb.title: Wrapping
breadcrumb.description: Breadcrumbs do not wrap by default. When horizontal space is limited, the last item is truncated with an ellipsis. Use the wrap attribute if you want the breadcrumb to wrap lines instead.
breadcrumb.code:
<m3e-breadcrumb wrap="">
  <m3e-breadcrumb-item>Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet</m3e-breadcrumb-item>
  <m3e-breadcrumb-item>Consectetur adipiscing elit sed do</m3e-breadcrumb-item>
  <m3e-breadcrumb-item>Tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore</m3e-breadcrumb-item>
  <m3e-breadcrumb-item>Magna aliqua ut enim ad minim veniam</m3e-breadcrumb-item>
  <m3e-breadcrumb-item> Quis nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris nisi ut aliquip </m3e-breadcrumb-item>
</m3e-breadcrumb>

calendar.title: Date selection
calendar.description: Set the date attribute to define the currently selected date. Listen for the change event to respond when the user selects a new one.

calendar.title: Start date
calendar.description: By default, a calendar opens to the selected date, or to today if no date is set. You can override this by using the start-at attribute to specify the date that m3e-calendar should initially display.

calendar.title: Start view
calendar.description: The start-view attribute can be used to set the view that will show up when the calendar first opens. It can be set to month, year, or multi-year; by default it will open to month view.

calendar.title: Date ranges
calendar.description: To select a range of dates instead of a single date, use the range-start and range-end attributes. Setting range-start puts the calendar into range-selection mode. When the user selects a date after the current range-start, both date and range-end are updated to that value. If the user selects a date before the current range-start, the range is reset and both date and range-start are set to the newly selected date.

calendar.title: Min and max dates
calendar.description: Use the min-date and max-date to disable all dates on the calendar before or after the respective values and prevent the user from advancing the calendar past the month or year (depending on current view) containing the minimum or maximum date.

calendar.title: Blackout dates
calendar.description: Use the blackoutDates property to specify a function used to determine whether a date is disabled and cannot be selected by the user.

calendar.title: Special dates
calendar.description: Use the specialDates property to specify a function used to determine whether a date has a highlighted look and feel. You can use the --m3e-calendar-item-special-container-color, --m3e-calendar-item-special-color, --m3e-calendar-item-special-ripple-color, --m3e-calendar-item-special-hover-color, and --m3e-calendar-item-special-focus-color CSS properties to style special dates.

checkbox.title: Basic usage
checkbox.description: Checkboxes may be standalone, pre-checked, or indeterminate. When clicked, a cancellable beforeinput event is emitted followed by an input and change event. To cancel checked state changes, call preventDefault during beforeinput.

checkbox.title: Labels
checkbox.description: Associate a label with a checkbox using the label element.

checkbox.title: Disabling
checkbox.description: Use the disabled attribute to disable a checkbox. Associated labels are automatically styled to show disabled.

checkbox.title: Required
checkbox.description: Use the required attribute to control whether a checkbox must be checked. The invalid event fires when a form is submitted and the element fails constraint validation. When invalid, labels are automatically styled accordingly.

chips.title: Non-interactive chips
chips.description: Use the m3e-chip component to present a non-interactive chip. You can optionally nest chips in a m3e-chip-set which provides default spacing between chips.

chips.title: Variants
chips.description: All chip types support two appearance variants: outlined (default) and elevated. Use the variant attribute to change a chip's appearance.

chips.title: Orientation
chips.description: Chips can be stacked vertically using the vertical attribute of a m3e-chip-set.

chips.title: Icons
chips.description: The m3e-chip provides slots for two icons. Use the icon slot to present an icon before a chip's label and use the trailing-icon slot to present an icon after it. While not required, the following example uses the m3e-icon component from @m3e/icon to present icons using Material Symbols.

chips.title: Assist chips
chips.description: Use the m3e-assist-chip to perform a smart or automated action that can span multiple applications.

chips.title: Icons (2)
chips.description: The m3e-assist-chip provides a single slot, icon, to present an icon before a chip's label. Trailing icons are not supported for this chip type.

chips.title: Disabling
chips.description: Use the disabled attribute to disable an assist chip. By default, disabled chips are not focusable with the keyboard. You can use the disabled-interactive attribute to support focusability of disabled controls to increase their discoverability.

chips.title: Links
chips.description: Assist chips can also behave like links by specifying a URL using the href attribute. You can also specify target, rel and download similar to native anchor tags.

chips.title: Filter chips
chips.description: Use the m3e-filter-chip and m3e-filter-chip-set components to present chips that users can select or deselect to filter content or data sets.

chips.title: Icons (3)
chips.description: When selected, a filter chip's icon is replaced with a check icon.

chips.title: Input chips
chips.description: The m3e-input-chip-set component raises a change event when chips are added to, or removed from, the set. Use the event's detail.type to determine whether the type of change was "add" or "remove". The event's detail also includes value and chip properties.
chips.code:
<m3e-form-field>
  <label slot="label" for="keywords">Keywords</label>
  <m3e-input-chip-set aria-label="Enter keywords">
    <input id="keywords" slot="input" placeholder="New keyword...">
  </m3e-input-chip-set>
</m3e-form-field>

chips.title: Prepopulated chips
chips.description: You can prepopulate a m3e-input-chip-set with chips using the m3e-input-chip component. Use the removable attribute to control whether a button is presented that allows the user to remove an input chip. This also controls whether BACKSPACE can be used to delete a chip using the keyboard.

<m3e-form-field>
  <label slot="label" for="keywords">Keywords</label>
  <m3e-input-chip-set aria-label="Enter keywords">
    <m3e-input-chip removable="">Design</m3e-input-chip>
    <m3e-input-chip removable="">Accessibility</m3e-input-chip>
    <m3e-input-chip removable="">Motion</m3e-input-chip>

</m3e-form-field>

chips.title: Suggestion chips
chips.description: Use the m3e-suggestion-chip component to offer users contextually relevant actions or responses. This component is visually and functionally equivalent to m3e-assist-chip, differing only in semantic intent. It encodes contextual suggestion rather than passive assistance, guiding user action through implied relevance.

chips.title: Icons (4)
chips.description: The m3e-suggestion-chip provides a single slot, icon, to present an icon before a chip's label. Trailing icons are not supported for this chip type.

chips.title: Disabling (5)
chips.description: Use the disabled attribute to disable a suggestion chip. By default, disabled chips are not focusable with the keyboard. You can use the disabled-interactive attribute to support focusability of disabled controls to increase their discoverability.

chips.title: Links (2)
chips.description: Suggestion chips can also behave like links by specifying a URL using the href attribute. You can also specify target, rel and download similar to native anchor tags.

chips.title: Density
chips.description: The following illustrates applying density to assist chips.
chips.code:
<m3e-assist-chip class="density-3">
  <m3e-icon slot="icon" name="stars"></m3e-icon>
  Density -3
</m3e-assist-chip>
<m3e-assist-chip class="density-2">
  <m3e-icon slot="icon" name="stars"></m3e-icon>
  Density -2
</m3e-assist-chip>
<m3e-assist-chip class="density-1">
  <m3e-icon slot="icon" name="stars"></m3e-icon>
  Density -1
</m3e-assist-chip>
<m3e-assist-chip class="density-0">
  <m3e-icon slot="icon" name="stars"></m3e-icon>
  Density 0
</m3e-assist-chip>

datepicker.title: Basic usage
datepicker.description: Use the m3e-datepicker-toggle to open a m3e-datepicker. This component should be nested inside a clickable element and be associated with a picker by setting the for attribute to the id of the picker to open.
datepicker.code: MM/DD/YYYY
datepicker.brevitySource: MM/DD/YYYY

datepicker.title: Variants
datepicker.description: All examples in this page use variant="auto".

datepicker.title: Date selection
datepicker.description: Use the date attribute to set the currently selected date, and listen for the change event to react when the user selects a new one. You can also enable the clearable attribute to display a button that clears the current selection.
datepicker.code: MM/DD/YYYY
datepicker.brevitySource:
<!-- Form field omitted for brevity -->
<m3e-datepicker id="datepicker" date="2026-01-01" clearable></m3e-datepicker>

datepicker.title: Start date
datepicker.description: By default, a datepicker opens to the selected date, or to today if no date is set. You can override this by using the start-at attribute to specify the date that m3e-datepicker should initially display.
datepicker.code: MM/DD/YYYY
datepicker.brevitySource:
<!-- Form field omitted for brevity -->
<m3e-datepicker id="datepicker" start-at="2026-01-01"></m3e-datepicker>

datepicker.title: Start view
datepicker.description: The start-view attribute can be used to set the view that will show up when the calendar first opens. It can be set to month, year, or multi-year; by default it will open to month view.
datepicker.code: MM/DD/YYYY
datepicker.brevitySource:
<!-- Form field omitted for brevity -->
<m3e-datepicker id="datepicker" start-view="multi-year"></m3e-datepicker>

datepicker.title: Date ranges
datepicker.description: To select a range of dates instead of a single date, use the range, or range-start and range-end attributes. Setting range or range-start puts the calendar into range-selection mode.
datepicker.code: MM/DD/YYYY - MM/DD/YYYY
datepicker.brevitySource: MM/DD/YYYY - MM/DD/YYYY

datepicker.title: Min and max dates
datepicker.description: Use the min-date and max-date to disable all dates on the calendar before or after the respective values and prevent the user from advancing the calendar past the month or year (depending on current view) containing the minimum or maximum date.
datepicker.code: MM/DD/YYYY
datepicker.brevitySource:
<!-- Form field omitted for brevity -->
<m3e-datepicker
  id="picker1"
  start-at="2026-04-01"
  min-date="2026-01-01"
  max-date="2026-04-30"
></m3e-datepicker>

datepicker.title: Blackout dates
datepicker.description: Use the blackoutDates property to specify a function used to determine whether a date is disabled and cannot be selected by the user.
datepicker.code: MM/DD/YYYY

datepicker.title: Special dates
datepicker.description: Use the specialDates property to specify a function used to determine whether a date has a highlighted look and feel. You can use the --m3e-calendar-item-special-container-color, --m3e-calendar-item-special-color, --m3e-calendar-item-special-ripple-color, --m3e-calendar-item-special-hover-color, and --m3e-calendar-item-special-focus-color CSS properties to style special dates.

fab.description: There are seven FAB appearance variants: primary, primary-container (default), secondary, and secondary-container, tertiary, tertiary-container, and surface. Use the variant attribute to change the appearance.
fab.code:
<m3e-fab variant="primary"><m3e-icon name="add"></m3e-icon></m3e-fab>
<m3e-fab variant="primary-container"><m3e-icon name="add"></m3e-icon></m3e-fab>
<m3e-fab variant="secondary"><m3e-icon name="add"></m3e-icon></m3e-fab>
<m3e-fab variant="secondary-container"><m3e-icon name="add"></m3e-icon></m3e-fab>
<m3e-fab variant="tertiary"><m3e-icon name="add"></m3e-icon></m3e-fab>

fab.description: Use the lowered attribute to lower the elevation of a FAB to reduce emphasis. Lowering is supported for all appearance variants.
fab.code:

<m3e-fab lowered="" variant="primary-container"><m3e-icon name="add"></m3e-icon></m3e-fab>
<m3e-fab lowered="" variant="secondary"><m3e-icon name="add"></m3e-icon></m3e-fab>
<m3e-fab lowered="" variant="secondary-container"><m3e-icon name="add"></m3e-icon></m3e-fab>
<m3e-fab lowered="" variant="tertiary"><m3e-icon name="add"></m3e-icon></m3e-fab>
<m3e-fab lowered="" variant="tertiary-container"><m3e-icon name="add"></m3e-icon></m3e-fab>
<m3e-fab lowered="" variant="surface"><m3e-icon name="add"></m3e-icon></m3e-fab>

</m3e-fab>

fab.title: Sizes

menu.title: Basic usage
menu.description: The m3e-menu is used in conjunction with a m3e-menu-trigger to create a menu opened when the parenting element of the trigger is activated. Use the for attribute of the m3e-menu-trigger to designate the menu to open.

menu.title: Variants
menu.description: There are two appearance variants: standard (default) and vibrant. Use the variant attribute to change the appearance.
menu.brevitySource:
<!-- Trigger omitted for brevity -->
<m3e-menu id="menu" variant="vibrant">
  <m3e-menu-item>Item 1</m3e-menu-item>
  <m3e-menu-item>Item 2</m3e-menu-item>
</m3e-menu>

menu.description: All menu item types provide two named slots for icons. Use the icon slot to present an icon before an item's label and use the trailing-icon slot to present an icon after it.

    Redial

  <m3e-menu-item>
    <m3e-icon slot="icon" name="voicemail"></m3e-icon>
    Check voice mail
  </m3e-menu-item>
  <m3e-menu-item>
    <m3e-icon slot="icon" name="notifications_off"></m3e-icon>
    Disable alerts
  </m3e-menu-item>
</m3e-menu>

menu.title: Checkboxes
menu.description: Use a m3e-menu-item-checkbox to present a toggleable menu item that supports multi-select. The checked attribute reflects the checked state of the menu item. If a menu item specifies an icon using the icon slot, it will be replaced with a check icon when checked.
menu.code:

  <m3e-menu-trigger for="menu2">Menu with checkboxes</m3e-menu-trigger>
</m3e-button>
<m3e-menu id="menu2">

    <m3e-icon slot="icon" name="format_bold"></m3e-icon>
    Bold
  </m3e-menu-item-checkbox>
  <m3e-menu-item-checkbox>
    <m3e-icon slot="icon" name="format_italic"></m3e-icon>
    Italic
  </m3e-menu-item-checkbox>
  <m3e-menu-item-checkbox>
    <m3e-icon slot="icon" name="format_underlined"></m3e-icon>
    Underline

</m3e-menu>

nav-bar.title: Items
nav-bar.description: As illustrated above, items are specified using the m3e-nav-item component. Use the default slot to specify an item's label and use the icon slot to specify an icon.

nav-bar.title: Selection
nav-bar.description: In addition, you can use the selected-icon slots to specify an alternate icon when selected. When using the m3e-icon to present icons, the icon will automatically be filled when an item is selected.

paginator.title: Page sizes

paginator.title: Navigation actions

progress-indicator.title: Determinate
progress-indicator.description: By default, circular progress indicators are determinate. Use the value attribute to indicate completion percentage and use max to specify the total number of steps or units of progress. In addition, you can provide content in the default slot.

progress-indicator.title: Indeterminate
progress-indicator.description: Use the indeterminate attribute to change the mode to indeterminate. In this mode, value and max are ignored.

progress-indicator.title: Determinate (2)
progress-indicator.description: When using the wavy variant, the indicator transitions to the flat appearance automatically when progress reaches 100%.

progress-indicator.title: Indeterminate (2)
progress-indicator.description: Use the indeterminate mode to express that something is happening, but without conveying discrete progress. In this mode, value and max are ignored.

progress-indicator.title: Buffer
progress-indicator.description: As with determinate mode, when using the wavy variant, the indicator transitions to the flat appearance automatically when progress reaches 100%.

progress-indicator.title: Query
progress-indicator.description: Use the query mode to indicate pre-loading before the actual loading starts. In this mode, value and max are ignored.

segmented-button.title: Anatomy
segmented-button.description: A segmented button is defined using the m3e-segmented-button component, which serves as a semantic container for a set of interactive segments. Each segment is represented by a m3e-button-segment component, allowing users to select from multiple related options within a unified control.
segmented-button.brevitySource:
<m3e-segmented-button>
  <m3e-button-segment checked>8 oz</m3e-button-segment>
  <!-- Additional segments omitted for brevity -->
</m3e-segmented-button>

segmented-button.title: Icons
segmented-button.description: When selected, a segments's icon is replaced with a check icon.
segmented-button.code:

segmented-button.title: Selection
segmented-button.description: Use the checked attribute to control the selected state of a m3e-button-segment. When clicked, a cancellable beforeinput event is emitted followed by an input and change event. To cancel selection change, call preventDefault during beforeinput.

  <m3e-button-segment checked>12 oz</m3e-button-segment>
  <m3e-button-segment>16 oz</m3e-button-segment>

</m3e-segmented-button>

select.title: Basic usage
select.description: To define selectable options within a m3e-select, include one or more m3e-option elements as its children. Each m3e-option may specify a value attribute, which determines the programmatic value submitted when the option is selected. The textual content of the m3e-option element represents the label presented to the user in the dropdown interface.

select.title: Empty options
select.description: To define an empty option, include a m3e-option without a value or content. Alternatively, set the value attribute to "" and include descriptive content such as "None". This signals that no meaningful value has been selected. Empty options are commonly used to represent a placeholder or default state prior to user interaction.

select.title: Option groups
select.description: Nested groups are not supported—only a single level of grouping is permitted.
select.code: Grass Bulbasaur Oddish Bellsprout Water Squirtle Psyduck Horsea Fire Charmander Vulpix Flareon

select.title: Selection
select.description: Use the selected attribute to control the selected state of a m3e-option. When clicked, a cancellable beforeinput event is emitted by the m3e-select followed by an input and change event. To cancel selection change, call preventDefault during beforeinput.
select.code:
<m3e-form-field>
  <label slot="label" for="select4">Toppings</label>
  <m3e-select id="select4" multi="">
    <m3e-option selected>Extra cheese</m3e-option>
    <m3e-option selected>Mushroom</m3e-option>
    <m3e-option>Onion</m3e-option>
    <m3e-option>Pepperoni</m3e-option>
    <m3e-option>Sausage</m3e-option>
    <m3e-option>Tomato</m3e-option>
  </m3e-select>
</m3e-form-field>

    <m3e-option>Oranges</m3e-option>
    <m3e-option>Bananas</m3e-option>
    <m3e-option>Grapes</m3e-option>
  </m3e-select>
</m3e-form-field>

select.description: Apply the required attribute to enforce selection. This ensures that users must choose at least one option before the field is considered valid. For multi-select configurations, at least one selected option satisfies the requirement.

    <m3e-option>Bananas</m3e-option>
    <m3e-option>Grapes</m3e-option>
  </m3e-select>
</m3e-form-field>

shape.description: It supports 31 shapes, specified using the name attribute: 4-leaf-clover 4-sided-cookie, 6-sided-cookie, 7-sided-cookie, 8-leaf-clover, 9-sided-cookie, 12-sided-cookie, arch, arrow, boom, bun, burst, circle, diamond, fan, flower, gem, ghost-ish, heart, hexagon, oval, pentagon, pill, pixel-circle, pixel-triangle, puffy, puffy-diamond, semicircle, slanted, soft-boom, soft-burst, square, sunny, triangle, and very-sunny.

shape.title: Images and video
shape.description: Nest images or videos inside a m3e-shape to clip them using the shape.

shape.title: Morphing
shape.description: All shape paths are normalized to the same number of points allowing shapes to be morphed using CSS clip-path transitions.

skeleton.title: Shape
skeleton.description: Use the shape attribute to define the geometry applied to all top level slotted content. Each node receives a corresponding skeleton overlay that reflects the selected shape. The circular option produces a circle based on the element size. The rounded option applies the standard radius token used by most surfaces and controls. The square option renders with no radius for a sharp edge appearance. The auto option reads the actual border radius of the slotted element and mirrors it exactly, ensuring the skeleton aligns with cards, buttons, images, or any custom component without additional configuration.

skeleton.title: Animation
skeleton.description: The none option disables motion and displays a static placeholder for environments that prefer reduced movement or for layouts that require a quiet loading state.

skeleton.title: Animation (2)
skeleton.description: The none option disables motion and displays a static placeholder for environments that prefer reduced movement or for layouts that require a quiet loading state.

slide-group.title: Basic usage
slide-group.description: To use the m3e-slide-group, simply nest content inside it. When content overflows, directional pagination buttons will appear allowing the user to click to scroll its content.

slide-group.title: Orientation
slide-group.description: By default, scroll groups are oriented horizontally. Use the vertical attribute to create a vertically oriented scroll group.
slide-group.brevitySource: Item 1

slider.title: Basic usage
slider.description: The m3e-slider allows for the selection of a value from a range similar to <input type="range">. They require a m3e-slider-thumb, which is responsible for rendering the interactive handle, managing pointer and keyboard input, and conveying current value semantics

slider.title: Selecting a value
slider.description: The thumb value is set to the minimum value unless otherwise specified using the value attribute of the m3e-slider-thumb. When changed through user interaction, a beforeinput event is emitted followed by an input and change event.

slider.title: Selecting a range
slider.description: A m3e-slider can be converted into a range slider by specifying two thumbs.

slider.title: Negative value ranges
slider.description: Negative value ranges are supported by setting the min attribute to a negative value.

slider.title: Sizes
slider.description: There are five size variants which can be used to add or remove emphasis: extra-small (default), small, medium, large, and extra-large. Use the size attribute to control a slider's size.

slider.title: Labels
slider.description: Use the labelled attribute of a m3e-slider to control whether to show thumb labels. By default, the value of a thumb is used as the label text. You can control label formatting by setting the displayWith property to a function that returns a label given a value.

slider.title: Tick marks
slider.description: By default, tick marks are not presented. Use the discrete attribute to show them. You must also specify a step larger than 1 for tick marks to show.

slider.title: Disabling
slider.description: While uncommon, individual thumbs can also be disabled while allowing another thumb to be enabled.

split-button.description: The following example illustrates use of the m3e-button for the leading button, m3e-icon-button for the trailing button, and m3e-menu for presenting a menu of secondary options.

    <m3e-icon name="keyboard_arrow_down"></m3e-icon>
    <m3e-menu-trigger for="menu"></m3e-menu-trigger>
  </m3e-icon-button>
</m3e-split-button>
<m3e-menu id="menu" position-x="before">

  <m3e-menu-item>Copy</m3e-menu-item>
  <m3e-menu-item>Delete</m3e-menu-item>
</m3e-menu>

split-pane.title: Basic usage
split-pane.description: The m3e-split-pane exposes two slots: start, which renders content on the logical start side of the layout, and end, which renders content on the logical end side. The value attribute controls the percentage of the available space occupied by the start pane, with the end pane automatically filling the remainder. By default, value is 50%. When a user interaction causes value to change, a cancellable beforeinput event is emitted, followed by an input and change event. This allows you to react to live resizing as well as commit final adjustments when the user finishes interacting with the drag handle.

split-pane.title: Orientation
split-pane.description: The m3e-split-pane supports three orientations: horizontal (default), vertical, and auto. Using auto, the orientation is responsive and determined by viewport size. Use the orientation attribute to change the orientation.

split-pane.title: Min and max sizes
split-pane.description: Use the min and max attributes to control the minimum and maximum percentage the start pane can occupy.

split-pane.title: Step size
split-pane.description: Use the step attribute to control how much the split pane's value changes with each keyboard resize action, such as pressing the arrow keys.

split-pane.title: Detents
split-pane.description: Double-clicking the drag handle, or pressing ENTER or SPACE when it is focused, will cycle to the next detent. Use the wrap-detents attribute to allow cycling to wrap from the last detent back to the first.

split-pane.title: Disabling
split-pane.description: Use the disabled attribute to disable resizing. When disabled, the drag handle is hidden from interaction, pointer and keyboard input are ignored, and the split pane remains fixed at its current value.

split-pane.title: Conditional rendering
split-pane.description: You can conditionally render the start or end pane either by omitting content from the corresponding slot or by applying the hidden attribute to slotted elements. When all assigned elements in a slot are hidden, the split pane treats that side as empty and updates the layout so the visible pane fully occupies the available space without modifying the current value.

split-pane.title: Nested panes
split-pane.description: Split panes can be nested to create complex layouts, allowing you to build multi-pane workspaces by combining multiple binary splits.
split-pane.code:
<m3e-split-pane value="25" class="complex">
  <m3e-card slot="start"></m3e-card>
  <m3e-split-pane slot="end" value="50" orientation="vertical">
    <m3e-card slot="start"></m3e-card>
    <m3e-card slot="end"></m3e-card>
  </m3e-split-pane>
</m3e-split-pane>

</m3e-split-pane>

stepper.title: Basic usage
stepper.description: To select a step, apply the selected attribute to a single m3e-step. When a step is activated, a cancellable beforeinput event is emitted followed by an input and change event. To cancel selection change, call preventDefault during beforeinput. Only one step may be selected at a time.

stepper.description: When using a horizontal stepper, you can control where the stepper's content is positioned using the header-position attribute. By default, the header is placed above content, but you can change it to below to placed it under the content.

stepper.title: Stepper buttons
stepper.description: Panels provide an actions slot for placing navigation controls. The m3e-stepper-previous, m3e-stepper-next, and m3e-stepper-reset components can be used to navigate steps. Using the m3e-stepper-reset will reset the stepper and any form data.

stepper.title: Linear stepper
stepper.description: A step is considered complete once the user transitions forward. Navigation back to an earlier step is allowed only when that step is explicitly designated as editable via the editable attribute.

switch.title: Basic usage
switch.description: Use the m3e-switch component to create a switch (also known as a slide toggle). Use the checked attribute to indicate whether the switch is "on". When clicked, a cancellable beforeinput event is emitted followed by an input andchange event. To cancel checked state changes, call preventDefault during beforeinput.

switch.title: Labels
switch.description: Associate a label with a switch using the label element.

switch.title: Icons
switch.description: You can control the icons to present using the icons attribute. Supports values include none (default), selected, and both.

switch.title: Disabling
switch.description: Use the disabled attribute to disable a switch. Associated labels are automatically styled to show disabled.
switch.code:
<label><m3e-switch disabled></m3e-switch> Disabled Switch 1</label>
<m3e-switch id="chk3" disabled></m3e-switch>
<label for="chk3"> Disabled Switch 2</label>

tabs.title: Basic usage
tabs.description: A tab's label is specified by its content. You can use the icon slot to render an icon before a tab's label.
tabs.code:

  <m3e-tab selected for="videos"><m3e-icon slot="icon" name="videocam"></m3e-icon>Video</m3e-tab>
  <m3e-tab for="photos"><m3e-icon slot="icon" name="photo"></m3e-icon>Photos</m3e-tab>
  <m3e-tab for="audio"><m3e-icon slot="icon" name="music_note"></m3e-icon>Audio</m3e-tab>
  <m3e-tab-panel id="videos">Videos</m3e-tab-panel>
  <m3e-tab-panel id="photos">Photos</m3e-tab-panel>
  <m3e-tab-panel id="audio">Audio</m3e-tab-panel>
</m3e-tabs>

<m3e-form-field variant="filled">

              <textarea id="field">

theme.title: Density
theme.description: The following example demonstrates how m3e-button responds to theme-level density adjustments. A medium-sized button is shown, which supports a minimum density of -2. Note that densities -3 and -2 produce identical layout behavior for this size, as values below the supported threshold are clamped to -2.
theme.code:
<m3e-theme strong-focus="" density="-3">
  <m3e-button variant="filled" size="medium">-3 density</m3e-button>
</m3e-theme>
<m3e-theme strong-focus="" density="-2">
  <m3e-button variant="filled" size="medium">-2 density</m3e-button>
</m3e-theme>
<m3e-theme strong-focus="" density="-1">
  <m3e-button variant="filled" size="medium">-1 density</m3e-button>
</m3e-theme>
<m3e-theme strong-focus="" density="0">
  <m3e-button variant="filled" size="medium">0 (default) density</m3e-button>
</m3e-theme>
<m3e-theme strong-focus="" density="1">
  <m3e-button variant="filled" size="medium">1 density</m3e-button>
</m3e-theme>

toc.title: Heading
toc.description: Use the overline and title slots to provide heading content for a TOC. The overline is limited to a single line and the title is limited to two lines. If content overflows, it will be truncated with an ellipsis.

tooltip.title: Plain tooltip
tooltip.description: Use the m3e-tooltip component to define a plain tooltip, and apply the for attribute to associate it with a specific UI element.

tooltip.title: Delays
tooltip.description: Use the show-delay and hide-delay attributes to control the amount of time, in milliseconds, to show or hide a tooltip. The following snippet illustrates the default delays.
tooltip.code:
<m3e-icon-button id="button">
  <m3e-icon name="arrow_back"></m3e-icon>
</m3e-icon-button>
<m3e-tooltip for="button" show-delay="0" hide-delay="200">Go Back</m3e-tooltip>

tooltip.title: Disabling
tooltip.description: Use the disabled attribute to disable a tooltip from showing.

tooltip.title: Rich tooltip
tooltip.description: Use the m3e-rich-tooltip component to define a rich tooltip, and apply the for attribute to associate it with a specific UI element. The subhead slot specifies the subhead content and the default slot specifies supplementary text.
tooltip.code: New settings available Now you can adjust the uploaded image quality, and upgrade your available storage space.
tooltip.brevitySource: New settings available Now you can adjust the uploaded image quality, and upgrade your available storage space.

tooltip.title: Actions
tooltip.description: You can include actions in a rich tooltip using the actions slot. In addition, use the m3e-rich-tooltip-action component, nested in a clickable element, to dismiss the tooltip. By default, focus is returned to the trigger. Use the disable-restore-focus attribute of a m3e-rich-tooltip-action to disable this behavior.
tooltip.code:

tree.title: Basic usage
tree.description: A basic tree consists of an m3e-tree component containing one or more m3e-tree-item. Items can be nested to create deeper levels in the hierarchy. Each item supplies its text through the label slot. Use the open attribute to control whether an item with nested children is expanded.
tree.code: Getting Started Overview Installation Components Button Card
tree.brevitySource: Getting Started Overview Installation

tree.title: Selection
tree.description: Use the selected attribute to mark an item as selected. The change event fires when the user changes selection, and the selected property provides access to the currently selected item(s).

tree.brevitySource: Getting Started Overview Installation

tree.title: Multiple selection
tree.description: Trees support both single-select and multi-select modes. Use the multi attribute of m3e-tree to allow multiple item to be selected simultaneously.
